senses.gloss: parasang, farsang; a linear measure, 500 قَصَبَة (qaṣaba) – about 1750 m
parasang, farsang; a linear measure, ¹⁄₈ of a مَرْحَلَة (marḥala) – 4830 m

etymology.text: From Northwestern Middle Iranian *frasax (“parasang”), cognate with Old Armenian հրասախ (hrasax) an Iranian loanword from same source. Compare Sorthwestern Iranian form, Middle Persian [Book Pahlavi needed] (plsng /⁠frasang⁠/) and Persian فرسنگ (farsang). Astronomical sense semantic loan from English parsec. See also فَرْسَخ فَلَكِيّ (farsaḵ falakiyy).
